ON WEDNESDAY EVENING, JUNE 1, 2022 AT APPROXIMATELY 7:05 PM, A MICROBURST WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WIND GUSTS OF 100 MPH OCCURRED JUST TO THE SOUTH OF SALEMBURG, NC. A MAJORITY OF THE DAMAGE OCCURRED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF OLD SALEMBURG ROAD AND LYMAN ROAD. SEVERAL SMALL OUTBUILDINGS WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED, WITH SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO TWO OTHER BARNS. NUMEROUS HARDWOOD TREES IN THIS AREA WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED OFF. TWO RESIDENTIAL HOMES ALSO SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE, MAINLY FROM LOSS OF SINGLES AND A WINDOW BLOWN OUT ALONG WITH A STORM DOOR DESTROYED. THE PATTERN OF THE DAMAGE WAS FANNED OUT, WITH THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE ALIGNED WITH THE STORM MOTION TO THE SOUTH. THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE WOULD LIKE TO THANK SAMPSON COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT AND THE SALEMBURG FIRE DEPARTMENT FOR THEIR ASSISTANCE WITH THE STORM SURVEY.
